revision.text: {{神社| 名称=雷神社| 画像=| 所在地=千葉県旭市見広1371| 祭神=[[天穂日命・別雷命]]| 社格=旧郷社| 創建=承平元年（931年）| == 祭神 == *[[天穂日命]]（あめのほひのみこと） *[[別雷命]]（わけいかづちのみこと） == 概要 == *第十二代景行天皇（在位は西暦七一～一三〇年）の御世に、天皇の皇子日本武尊（やまとたけるのみこと）が東国を平定し、大和の国に帰る途中、伊勢の能褒野（のぼの）で崩御。天皇は日本武尊を追慕し、東征の跡を御巡幸されたおり、椿の湖の東端に東国鎮守として雷神社を西暦１２３年に創建。現在の社殿は明治四十三年に完成したものである。境内社としては、津島大神（享和四年）、田の神諸神（文政十一年）、天王宮（安政二年）、石尊大権現（安永四年）、金毘羅神（文化二年）、疱瘡神（天保十五年）、天満宮（昭和五十二年）、稲荷神社（平成十九年移設）がある。 == 交通 == *[[ＪＲ総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩１５分。 == 外部リンク == *http://raijinjya.jp/

{{神社| 名称=雷神社| 画像=| 所在地=千葉県旭市1371| 祭神=[[天穂日命・別雷命]]| 社格=旧郷社| }}
== 祭神 == *[[天穂日命]]（あめのほひのみこと） *[[別雷命]]（わけいかづちのみこと） == 概要 == *第十二代景行天皇（在位は西暦七一～一三〇年）の御世に、天皇の皇子日本武尊（やまとたけるのみこと）が東国を平定し、大和の国に帰る途中、伊勢の能褒野（のぼの）で崩御。天皇は日本武尊を追慕し、東征の跡を御巡幸されたおり、椿の湖の東端に東国鎮守として雷神社を西暦１２３年に創建。現在の社殿は明治四十三年に完成したものである。境内社としては、津島大神（享和四年）、田の神諸神（文政十一年）、天王宮（安政二年）、石尊大権現（安永四年）、金毘羅神（文化二年）、疱瘡神（天保十五年）、天満宮（昭和五十二年）、稲荷神社（平成十九年移設）がある。 == 交通 == *[[ＪＲ総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩１５分。 == 外部リンク == *http://raijinjya.jp/

{{神社| 名称=雷神社| 画像=| 所在地=千葉県旭市1371| 祭神=[[天穂日命・別雷命]]| 社格=旧郷社| }} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は[[千葉県]][[旭市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == *[[天穂日命]]（あめのほひのみこと） *[[別雷命]]（わけいかづちのみこと） == 概要 == *第十二代景行天皇（在位は西暦七一～一三〇年）の御世に、天皇の皇子日本武尊（やまとたけるのみこと）が東国を平定し、大和の国に帰る途中、伊勢の能褒野（のぼの）で崩御。天皇は日本武尊を追慕し、東征の跡を御巡幸されたおり、椿の湖の東端に東国鎮守として雷神社を西暦１２３年に創建。現在の社殿は明治四十三年に完成したものである。境内社としては、津島大神（享和四年）、田の神諸神（文政十一年）、天王宮（安政二年）、石尊大権現（安永四年）、金毘羅神（文化二年）、疱瘡神（天保十五年）、天満宮（昭和五十二年）、稲荷神社（平成十九年移設）がある。 == 交通 == *[[ＪＲ総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩１５分。 == 外部リンク == *http://raijinjya.jp/
{{デフォルトソート:らいしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]]

{{神社 |名称=雷神社 |画像= |所在地=千葉県旭市見広1371 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天穂日命]]・[[別雷命]] |社格=旧郷社 |}} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は[[千葉県]][[旭市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == *[[天穂日命]]（あめのほひのみこと） *[[別雷命]]（わけいかづちのみこと）
== 概要 == *第十二代[[景行天皇]]（在位は西暦71～130年）の御世に、天皇の皇子[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]（やまとたけるのみこと）が東国を平定し、大和の国に帰る途中、伊勢の能褒野（のぼの）で崩御。天皇は日本武尊を追慕し、東征の跡を御巡幸されたおり、椿の湖の東端に東国鎮守として雷神社を西暦[[123年]]に創建。現在の社殿は明治四十三年に完成したものである。境内社としては、津島大神（享和四年）、田の神諸神（文政十一年）、天王宮（安政二年）、石尊大権現（安永四年）、金毘羅神（文化二年）、疱瘡神（天保十五年）、天満宮（昭和五十二年）、稲荷神社（平成十九年移設）がある。
== 交通 == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩15分。
== 外部リンク == *[http://raijinjya.jp/ 公式サイト]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:らいしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]]

{{神社 |名称=雷神社 |画像= |所在地=千葉県旭市見広1371 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天穂日命]]・[[別雷命]] |社格=旧郷社 |}} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は[[千葉県]][[旭市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == *[[天穂日命]]（あめのほひのみこと） *[[別雷命]]（わけいかづちのみこと）
== 概要 == *第十二代[[景行天皇]]（在位は西暦71～130年）の御世に、天皇の皇子[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]（やまとたけるのみこと）が東国を平定し、大和の国に帰る途中、伊勢の能褒野（のぼの）で崩御。天皇は日本武尊を追慕し、東征の跡を御巡幸されたおり、椿の湖の東端に東国鎮守として雷神社を西暦[[123年]]に創建。現在の社殿は明治四十三年に完成したものである。境内社としては、津島大神（享和四年）、田の神諸神（文政十一年）、天王宮（安政二年）、石尊大権現（安永四年）、金毘羅神（文化二年）、疱瘡神（天保十五年）、天満宮（昭和五十二年）、稲荷神社（平成十九年移設）がある。
== 交通 == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩15分。
== 外部リンク == *[http://raijinjya.jp/ 公式サイト]

revision.comment: 様式は[[東大社]]に準ずる。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=雷神社 |画像= |所在地=[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広1371 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]・[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格=郷社 |創建=景行天皇53年 |別名= |本殿= |例祭=[[6月15日]] |神事= }} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広（[[下総国]][[海上郡]]）にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。
[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]]を配祀する。
== 概要 == [[伊勢国]]の能褒野で崩御した皇子[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を追慕して、[[景行天皇]]が東国巡幸をした折、[[椿海]]の東端に一社を造営東海の鎮護としたことが当社の始まりとされ、その後[[下海上国造]]に任じられた久都伎直が、自らの祖神である天穂日命を奉祀したものと伝えられている。[[延暦]]12年（[[793年]]）には、[[賀茂別雷神社]]より「別雷命」を[[勧請]]して配祀し、[[桓武天皇]]より「雷大神」の称号を賜ったといわれている。
現在の社殿は[[明治]]43年（[[1910年]]）に完成したものである。境内社としては、[[享和]]4年（[[1804年]]）創建の[[津島信仰|津島大神]]、[[文政]]11年（[[1828年]]）の[[田の神]]諸神、[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）の天王宮、[[安永 (元号)|安永]]4年（[[1775年]]）の石尊大権現、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年（[[1805年]]）の[[金毘羅権現|金毘羅神]]、[[天保]]15年（[[1844年]]）の疱瘡神、[[昭和]]52年（[[1977年]]）の[[天満宮]]、[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）移設の[[稲荷神|稲荷神社]]がある。
[[東大社]]、[[豊玉姫神社 (香取市)|豊玉姫神社]]とともに、20年目毎に催す式年大神幸祭で[[神輿]]の[[露払い]]として舞われる「倉橋の弥勒三番叟」は、[[鹿島踊り]]の一形態ともされ一列になって進みながら舞う形態が希少であることから、[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|千葉県の無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== 交通 == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩15分。
== 外部リンク == *[http://raijinjya.jp/ 公式サイト]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:らいしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:旭市]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称=雷神社 |画像= |所在地=[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広1371 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]・[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格=郷社 |創建=景行天皇53年 |別名= |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[6月15日]] |神事= }} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広（[[下総国]][[海上郡]]）にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。
[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]]を配祀する。
== 概要 == [[伊勢国]]の能褒野で崩御した皇子[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を追慕して、[[景行天皇]]が東国巡幸をした折、[[椿海]]の東端に一社を造営東海の鎮護としたことが当社の始まりとされ、その後[[下海上国造]]に任じられた久都伎直が、自らの祖神である天穂日命を奉祀したものと伝えられている。[[延暦]]12年（[[793年]]）には、[[賀茂別雷神社]]より「別雷命」を[[勧請]]して配祀し、[[桓武天皇]]より「雷大神」の称号を賜ったといわれている。
現在の社殿は[[明治]]43年（[[1910年]]）に完成したものである。境内社としては、[[享和]]4年（[[1804年]]）創建の[[津島信仰|津島大神]]、[[文政]]11年（[[1828年]]）の[[田の神]]諸神、[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）の天王宮、[[安永 (元号)|安永]]4年（[[1775年]]）の石尊大権現、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年（[[1805年]]）の[[金毘羅権現|金毘羅神]]、[[天保]]15年（[[1844年]]）の[[疱瘡神]]、[[昭和]]52年（[[1977年]]）の[[天満宮]]、[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）移設の[[稲荷神|稲荷神社]]がある。
[[東大社]]、[[豊玉姫神社 (香取市)|豊玉姫神社]]とともに20年目毎に催す[[式年祭|式年]][[神幸祭]]で、[[神輿]]の[[露払い]]として舞われる「倉橋の弥勒三番叟」は、[[鹿島踊り]]の一形態ともされ一列になって進みながら舞う形態が希少であることから、[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|千葉県の無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩15分。
== 参考文献 == * 『全国神社名鑑 上巻』全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター、昭和52年。 * 『千葉県の地名（日本歴史地名大系 12）』[[平凡社]]、1996年。ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1
== 外部リンク == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ 雷神社 公式サイト] * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1661/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 式年三社銚子大神幸祭（旭市）] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html 銚子市観光協会 式年銚子大神幸祭]

{{神社 |名称=雷神社 |画像= |所在地=[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広1371 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]・[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格=郷社 |創建=景行天皇53年 |別名= |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[6月15日]] |神事= }} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広（[[下総国]][[海上郡]]）にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。

[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]]を配祀する。
== 概要 == [[景行天皇]]が、[[伊勢国]]の能褒野で崩御した皇子[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を追慕して東国巡幸をした折、[[椿海]]の東端に一社を造営東海の鎮護としたことが当社の始まりとされ、その後[[下海上国造]]に任じられた久都伎直が、自らの祖神である天穂日命を奉祀したものと伝えられている。[[延暦]]12年（[[793年]]）には、[[賀茂別雷神社]]より「別雷命」を[[勧請]]して配祀し、[[桓武天皇]]より「雷大神」の称号を賜ったといわれている。
現在の社殿は[[明治]]43年（[[1910年]]）に完成したものである。境内社としては、[[享和]]4年（[[1804年]]）創建の[[津島信仰|津島大神]]、[[文政]]11年（[[1828年]]）の[[田の神]]諸神、[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）の天王宮、[[安永 (元号)|安永]]4年（[[1775年]]）の石尊大権現、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年（[[1805年]]）の[[金毘羅権現|金毘羅神]]、[[天保]]15年（[[1844年]]）の[[疱瘡神]]、[[昭和]]52年（[[1977年]]）の[[天満宮]]、[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）移設の[[稲荷神|稲荷神社]]がある。
== 行事 == [[東大社]]、[[豊玉姫神社 (香取市)|豊玉姫神社]]とともに20年目毎に催す[[式年祭|式年]][[神幸祭]]で、[[神輿]]の[[露払い]]として舞われる「倉橋の弥勒三番叟」は、[[鹿島踊り]]の一形態ともされ一列になって進みながら舞う形態が希少であることから、[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|千葉県の無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩15分。
== 参考文献 == * 『全国神社名鑑 上巻』全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター、昭和52年。 * 『千葉県の地名（日本歴史地名大系 12）』[[平凡社]]、1996年。ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1

{{神社 |名称=雷神社 |画像=[[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|雷神社（旭市）拝殿]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |所在地=[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広1371 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]・[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格=旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[景行天皇]]53年 |別名= |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[6月15日]] |神事= }} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広（[[下総国]][[海上郡]]）にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。 [[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]]を配祀する。 == 概要 == [[景行天皇]]が、[[伊勢国]]の能褒野で崩御した皇子[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を追慕して東国巡幸をした折、[[椿海]]の東端に一社を造営東海の鎮護としたことが当社の始まりとされ、その後[[下海上国造]]に任じられた久都伎直が、自らの祖神である天穂日命を奉祀したものと伝えられている。[[延暦]]12年（[[793年]]）には、[[賀茂別雷神社]]より「別雷命」を[[勧請]]して配祀し、[[桓武天皇]]より「雷大神」の称号を賜ったといわれている。 現在の社殿は[[明治]]43年（[[1910年]]）に完成したものである。境内社としては、[[享和]]4年（[[1804年]]）創建の[[津島信仰|津島大神]]、[[文政]]11年（[[1828年]]）の[[田の神]]諸神、[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）の天王宮、[[安永 (元号)|安永]]4年（[[1775年]]）の石尊大権現、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年（[[1805年]]）の[[金毘羅権現|金毘羅神]]、[[天保]]15年（[[1844年]]）の[[疱瘡神]]、[[昭和]]52年（[[1977年]]）の[[天満宮]]、[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）移設の[[稲荷神|稲荷神社]]がある。 == 行事 == [[東大社]]、[[豊玉姫神社 (香取市)|豊玉姫神社]]とともに20年目毎に催す[[式年祭|式年]][[神幸祭]]で、[[神輿]]の[[露払い]]として舞われる「倉橋の弥勒三番叟」は、[[鹿島踊り]]の一形態ともされ一列になって進みながら舞う形態が希少であることから、[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|千葉県の無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『全国神社名鑑 上巻』全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター、昭和52年。 * 『千葉県の地名（日本歴史地名大系 12）』[[平凡社]]、1996年。ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == 外部リンク == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ 雷神社 公式サイト] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 雷神社] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html 銚子市観光協会 式年銚子大神幸祭] {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:らいしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |所在地=[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広1371 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]・[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格=旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[景行天皇]]53年 |別名= |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[6月15日]] |神事= }} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広（[[下総国]][[海上郡]]）にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。 [[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]]を配祀する。 == 概要 == [[景行天皇]]が、[[伊勢国]]の能褒野で崩御した皇子[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を追慕して東国巡幸をした折、[[椿海]]の東端に一社を造営東海の鎮護としたことが当社の始まりとされ、その後[[下海上国造]]に任じられた久都伎直が、自らの祖神である天穂日命を奉祀したものと伝えられている。[[延暦]]12年（[[793年]]）には、[[賀茂別雷神社]]より「別雷命」を[[勧請]]して配祀し、[[桓武天皇]]より「雷大神」の称号を賜ったといわれている。 現在の社殿は[[明治]]43年（[[1910年]]）に完成したものである。境内社としては、[[享和]]4年（[[1804年]]）創建の[[津島信仰|津島大神]]、[[文政]]11年（[[1828年]]）の[[田の神]]諸神、[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）の天王宮、[[安永 (元号)|安永]]4年（[[1775年]]）の石尊大権現、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年（[[1805年]]）の[[金毘羅権現|金毘羅神]]、[[天保]]15年（[[1844年]]）の[[疱瘡神]]、[[昭和]]52年（[[1977年]]）の[[天満宮]]、[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）移設の[[稲荷神|稲荷神社]]がある。 == 行事 == [[東大社]]、[[豊玉姫神社 (香取市)|豊玉姫神社]]とともに20年目毎に催す[[式年祭|式年]][[神幸祭]]で、[[神輿]]の[[露払い]]として舞われる「倉橋の弥勒三番叟」は、[[鹿島踊り]]の一形態ともされ一列になって進みながら舞う形態が希少であることから、[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|千葉県の無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『全国神社名鑑 上巻』全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター、昭和52年。 * 『千葉県の地名（日本歴史地名大系 12）』[[平凡社]]、1996年。ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == 外部リンク == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ 雷神社 公式サイト] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 雷神社] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html 銚子市観光協会 式年銚子大神幸祭] {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:らいしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

[[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] == Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|ja|倉橋駅}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|ja|総武本線}}, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[ja:雷神社]]

Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}}, Kaijō District. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|ja|倉橋駅}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|ja|総武本線}}, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[ja:雷神社]]

Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|ja|倉橋駅}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|ja|総武本線}}, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雷神社]]

[[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}}[[Category:11]] == Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|ja|倉橋駅}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|ja|総武本線}}, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:11]][[en:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]]

{{citation needed|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}} {{citation needed|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}} == Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|ja|倉橋駅}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|ja|総武本線}}, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{citation needed|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}} {{citation needed|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}} {{citation needed|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}} {{citation needed|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}} {{citation needed|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:雷神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)}}

{{神社 |名称=雷神社 |画像=[[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|雷神社（旭市）拝殿]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |所在地=[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広1371 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]・[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格=旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[景行天皇]]53年 |別名= |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[6月15日]] |神事= }} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広（[[下総国]][[海上郡]]）にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。 [[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]]を配祀する。 == 概要 == [[景行天皇]]が、[[伊勢国]]の能褒野で崩御した皇子[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を追慕して東国巡幸をした折、[[椿海]]の東端に一社を造営東海の鎮護としたことが当社の始まりとされ、その後[[下海上国造]]に任じられた久都伎直が、自らの祖神である天穂日命を奉祀したものと伝えられている。[[延暦]]12年（[[793年]]）には、[[賀茂別雷神社]]より「別雷命」を[[勧請]]して配祀し、[[桓武天皇]]より「雷大神」の称号を賜ったといわれている。 現在の社殿は[[明治]]43年（[[1910年]]）に完成したものである。境内社としては、[[享和]]4年（[[1804年]]）創建の[[津島信仰|津島大神]]、[[文政]]11年（[[1828年]]）の[[田の神]]諸神、[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）の天王宮、[[安永 (元号)|安永]]4年（[[1775年]]）の石尊大権現、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年（[[1805年]]）の[[金毘羅権現|金毘羅神]]、[[天保]]15年（[[1844年]]）の[[疱瘡神]]、[[昭和]]52年（[[1977年]]）の[[天満宮]]、[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）移設の[[稲荷神|稲荷神社]]がある。 == 行事 == [[東大社]]、[[豊玉姫神社 (香取市)|豊玉姫神社]]とともに20年目毎に催す[[式年祭|式年]][[神幸祭]]で、[[神輿]]の[[露払い]]として舞われる「倉橋の弥勒三番叟」は、[[鹿島踊り]]の一形態ともされ一列になって進みながら舞う形態が希少であることから、[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|千葉県の無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『全国神社名鑑 上巻』全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター、昭和52年。 * 『千葉県の地名（日本歴史地名大系 12）』[[平凡社]]、1996年。ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == 外部リンク == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ 雷神社 公式サイト] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 雷神社] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html 銚子市観光協会 式年銚子大神幸祭] {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:らいしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[雷神社 (旭市)]] to [[Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=雷神社 |画像=[[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|雷神社（旭市）拝殿]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |所在地=[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広1371 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]・[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格=旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[景行天皇]]53年 |別名= |本殿=流造 |例祭=[[6月15日]] |神事= }} '''雷神社'''（らいじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[旭市]]見広（[[下総国]][[海上郡]]）にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]。 [[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]]を配祀する。 == 概要 == [[景行天皇]]が、[[伊勢国]]の能褒野で崩御した皇子[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を追慕して東国巡幸をした折、[[椿海]]の東端に一社を造営東海の鎮護としたことが当社の始まりとされ、その後[[下海上国造]]に任じられた久都伎直が、自らの祖神である天穂日命を奉祀したものと伝えられている。[[延暦]]12年（[[793年]]）には、[[賀茂別雷神社]]より「別雷命」を[[勧請]]して配祀し、[[桓武天皇]]より「雷大神」の称号を賜ったといわれている。 現在の社殿は[[明治]]43年（[[1910年]]）に完成したものである。境内社としては、[[享和]]4年（[[1804年]]）創建の[[津島信仰|津島大神]]、[[文政]]11年（[[1828年]]）の[[田の神]]諸神、[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）の天王宮、[[安永 (元号)|安永]]4年（[[1775年]]）の石尊大権現、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年（[[1805年]]）の[[金毘羅権現|金毘羅神]]、[[天保]]15年（[[1844年]]）の[[疱瘡神]]、[[昭和]]52年（[[1977年]]）の[[天満宮]]、[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）移設の[[稲荷神|稲荷神社]]がある。 == 行事 == [[東大社]]、[[豊玉姫神社 (香取市)|豊玉姫神社]]とともに20年目毎に催す[[式年祭|式年]][[神幸祭]]で、[[神輿]]の[[露払い]]として舞われる「倉橋の弥勒三番叟」は、[[鹿島踊り]]の一形態ともされ一列になって進みながら舞う形態が希少であることから、[[千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|千葉県の無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[総武本線]][[倉橋駅]]より徒歩15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『全国神社名鑑 上巻』全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター、昭和52年。 * 『千葉県の地名（日本歴史地名大系 12）』[[平凡社]]、1996年。ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == 外部リンク == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ 雷神社 公式サイト] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ ちばの観光まるごと紹介 雷神社] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html 銚子市観光協会 式年銚子大神幸祭] {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:らいしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]]

== Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|ja|倉橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|ja|総武本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:雷神社]]

== Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kaijō District. Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kaijō District. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|ja|倉橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|ja|総武本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:雷神社]]

== Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimōsa Province|qq=}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmu|qq=}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokami|qq=}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōsōgami|qq=}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangū|qq=}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|ja|倉橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|ja|総武本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Main Line|qq=}}, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:雷神社]]

Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimōsa Province|qq=}}, Kaijō District. Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokami|qq=}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōsōgami|qq=}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangū|qq=}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|ja|倉橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|ja|総武本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Main Line|qq=}}, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:雷神社]]

== Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimōsa Province|qq=}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmu|qq=}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Tanokami|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokami|qq=}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Hōsōgami|ja|疱瘡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōsōgami|qq=}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangū|qq=}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=}} every 20 years. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=}} every 20 years. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]]

The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Asahi, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]]

== Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996.
