Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]]

== Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:旭市の歴史]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]]

In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:旭市の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]]

== Raijin Shrine == {{shrine |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[ja:%E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]]

Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[sn:E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[sn:E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[sn:E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11660190]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]]}} [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Asahi City)]] [[sn:E9%9B%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%97%AD%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11660190]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11660190}}

The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}}

Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|qq=|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|qq=|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|qq=|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}}

In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}}

[[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660190]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}}
