{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660190]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660190]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660190]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]] Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mihiro, [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], in the former [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]], Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:雷神社]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] and enshrined alongside, with Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Tanokami]] deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * [[:en:Smallpox demon|Hōsōgami]] (1844, Tenpō 15) * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] (1977, Shōwa 52) * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kurahashi Station|Kurahashi Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]], operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷神社]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660190]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Kanmu}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Tanokami}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=Hōsōgami}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|en|Kurahashi Station|WD=Q5364298|lt=Kurahashi Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line}}, operated by {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷神社]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660190]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Kanmu}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Tanokami}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=Hōsōgami}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|en|Kurahashi Station|WD=Q5364298|lt=Kurahashi Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line}}, operated by {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷神社]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Tanokami}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=Hōsōgami}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|en|Kurahashi Station|WD=Q5364298|lt=Kurahashi Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line}}, operated by {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷神社]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakinoumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Kanmu}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Tanokami}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=Hōsōgami}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] == Raijin Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name = Raijin Shrine |image = [[File:雷神社（旭市）.jpg|250px|Raijin Shrine (Asahi City) Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}} Mihiro 1371 |coordinates = {{coord|35|44|27.3|N|140|41|48.9|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity = {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |status = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = 53rd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} |main hall = Nagare-zukuri |annual festival = June 15 }} '''Raijin Shrine''' (雷神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mihiro, {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, in the former {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}, Kaijō District. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The primary enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, with {{ill|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo-wake-ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} also worshipped. == Overview == According to legend, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, mourning the death of his son {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} in Nobono, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}, constructed a shrine at the eastern edge of {{ill|Tsubaki Sea|ja|椿海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Sea|1=Tsubakimi Lake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5120787}} to protect the eastern sea. Later, {{ill|Kutsuki no Atae|ja|久都伎直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, appointed as the ruler of Lower Kaijō Province, enshrined his ancestral deity, Ame-no-hohi. In 793 (Enryaku 12), the deity "Wakeraijin" was invited from {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and enshrined alongside, with Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} granting the title "Raijin-ōkami." The current shrine building was completed in 1910 (Meiji 43). Subsidiary shrines include: * {{ill|Tsushima Ōkami|ja|津島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (founded 1804, Kyōwa 4) * Various {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Tanokami|1=Ta-no-Kami}} deities (1828, Bunsei 11) * {{ill|Tennōgū|ja|天王宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōgū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天王神社|1=Tenno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443028}} (1855, Ansei 2) * {{ill|Sekison Daigongen|ja|石尊大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石尊権現|1=Sekison Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55528104}} (1775, An'ei 4) * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (1805, Bunka 2) * {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=Hōsōgami|1=Smallpox demon}} (1844, Tenpō 15) * {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}} (1977, Shōwa 52) * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} (relocated in 2007, Heisei 19) == Festivals and Events == Together with {{ill|Tōdaisha|ja|東大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaisha|1=Tōdai-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526492}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|ja|豊玉姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime Shrine (Katori)|1=Q11633963|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633963}}, Raijin Shrine hosts a {{ill|shikinen shinkōsai|ja|式年神幸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikinen shinkōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} every 20 years. The "Kurahashi no Miroku Sambasō" dance, performed as a forerunner to the portable shrine procession, is considered a form of {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}. Its unique formation of dancers moving in a line has led to its designation as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Q11406175|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kurahashi Station|en|Kurahashi Station|WD=Q5364298|lt=Kurahashi Station|1=Kurahashi Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line|1=Sōbu Main Line}}, operated by {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}. == References == * "Zenkoku Jinja Meikan, Volume 1," Zenkoku Jinja Meikan Kankōkai Shigaku Center, 1977 (Shōwa 52). * "Chiba-ken no Chimei" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 == External Links == * [http://raijinjya.jp/ Official Website of Raijin Shrine] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/3047/ Raijin Shrine - Chiba Tourism Information] * [http://www.choshikanko.com/jinkosai/index.html Choshi City Tourism Association - Shikinen Choshi Daijin Shinkōsai] {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (旭市)|version=101901555|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷神社]] [[ja:雷神社 (旭市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660189}} [[Category:Architecture of Asahi City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Asahi (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
